# ✅ Shipment Ingestion

# **Continuous Glucose Monitoring Shipment Ingestion**

**Author:** [Anne Oursler](mailto:aoursler@verily.com)  
**Date:** Jan 29, 2026   
**Team:** Devices  
**Related Epic:** [ONEVERILY-107282](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-107282)  
**Launch:** None Needed

| Reviewer | Status | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| [Maria Zakhalyavko](mailto:mariaoz@verily.com) | Completed | LGTM\! |
| [Arjun Gupta](mailto:guptarju@verily.health) | Completed |  |
| [Jerome Adam-Cote](mailto:jeromeadamcote@verily.health) | Completed | Feb 10, 2026 |
| [Kara Yimoyines](mailto:karayim@verily.com) | Completed | LGTM |

### Haiku by [Noah Adler](mailto:ndadler@verily.health):

| *Code you tell me how? I code do better than that Shipment ingestion* |
| :---- |

# Open Questions

* What are the new salesforce codes representing these CGMs which we want to ingest?  
  * G7 10 day wear \= **PDXCG7SENTR1**  
  * G7 15 day wear \= **PDXSTPFT011**  
  * Stelo \= **PDSTPXN002**  
* Can we also include the scale code ( **0383** ) which we began shipping but never added to the pipeline?  
* Confirming that billing only needs the SupplyDelivery resources, not any Device resource since these devices will integrate through HK and HC.

# Overview

We currently have two consecutive pipelines which first get shipment data from Salesforce into the landing FHIR store (sfo-lan-shp) and then from the landing FHIR store into the operational FHIR store (lan-op-shp). The first pipeline primarily brings data into the OneVerily eco-system, and the second ensures it’s properly mapped and all needed resources are created. As part of that process we only write supply delivery resources with salesforce order codes which we recognize and will be able to write to the operational FHIR store.   

## Note:

[Arjun Gupta](mailto:guptarju@verily.com)has confirmed that this change should not require a launch as it is trivial in nature. This will greatly accelerate the start of our CGM work.

# Architecture

![][image1]

# Design

## sfo-lan-shp Pipeline

Our sfo-lan-shp pipeline has configs per environment for the product codes we will ingest. We’ll need to append our new codes in this list. These lists can be found in each environment at the following github urls:

* Dev-Stable:[https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/ingestion/ingest-request/requests/dev-stable/pipeline/update/sfo-lan-shp.yaml\#L10-L40](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/ingestion/ingest-request/requests/dev-stable/pipeline/update/sfo-lan-shp.yaml#L10-L40)  
* Test:[https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/ingestion/ingest-request/requests/test/pipeline/update/sfo-lan-shp.yaml\#L10-L40](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/ingestion/ingest-request/requests/test/pipeline/update/sfo-lan-shp.yaml#L10-L40)  
* Prod:[https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/ingestion/ingest-request/requests/prod/pipeline/update/sfo-lan-shp.yaml\#L10-L40](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/ingestion/ingest-request/requests/prod/pipeline/update/sfo-lan-shp.yaml#L10-L40)

## FHIR IG Code Systems

Likewise each code is stored in a FHIR IG code system, and without it FHIR will reject writing the SupplyDelivery resource. Therefore we should update [https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/cortex/fhir-ig/core/input/fsh/codeSystems/SalesforceItemCodes.fsh](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/cortex/fhir-ig/core/input/fsh/codeSystems/SalesforceItemCodes.fsh). Please note that these changes should be done well in advance of the updates above to avoid release bottlenecks, and so have quietly been done alongside this Design.

# Testing

Existing Unit and Integration tests largely already cover this behavior, and we can extend them to verify that the new code specifically is being mapped.  
For example:  
[https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/tree/main/ingestion/module/mapping/salesforceshipment/mapping/testdata](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/tree/main/ingestion/module/mapping/salesforceshipment/mapping/testdata)

```json
 {                                                                      
    "salesforceId": "test-order-123",                                    
    "memberId": "https://env.one.verily.com/cortex-fhir-proxy/operational
  /fhir/Patient/456",                                                    
    "memberOrganization": "https://env.one.verily.com/cortex-fhir-proxy/o
  perational/fhir/Organization/123",                                     
    "status": "Order Sent to Distributor",                               
    "createdDate": "2024-04-19T13:11:11.000Z",                           
    "shippingCity": "Boston",                                            
    "shippingState": "MA",                                               
    "shippingPostalCode": "12345",                                       
    "shippingCountry": "US",                                             
    "shippingStreet": "123 Test St",                                     
    "orderItems": [                                                      
      {                                                                  
        "salesforceId": "test-item-456",                                 
        "productCode": "YOUR_NEW_CODE_1",                                
        "productName": "New Supply Type",                                
        "quantity": 1,                                                   
        "status": "Shipped",                                             
        "trackingNumber": "1234567890123",                               
        "createdDate": "2024-04-19T13:11:43.000Z",                       
        "closeDate": "2024-04-19T15:05:00.000Z"                          
      }                                                                  
    ]                                                                    
  }
```

Additionally to test the mapping function specifically we can add a new status code to a scenario of [https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/ingestion/module/mapping/salesforceshipment/mapping/features/orderitem\_to\_supplydelivery.feature\#L19](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/ingestion/module/mapping/salesforceshipment/mapping/features/orderitem_to_supplydelivery.feature#L19)

# Task Breakdown

**Epic:** [ONEVERILY-107282](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-107282)

| Task | Points(by complexity) |
| :---- | :---- |
| Add code to pipeline configs | 1 |
| Add code to fsh code list | 1 |
| Test new code | 2 |

# Privacy and Security Considerations

SupplyDelivery resources are already being ingested from Salesforce and the addition of a code to support ingesting CGMs is not expected to add additional Privacy or Security concerns.

# ✅ FHIR Writes

# **Continuous Glucose Monitoring**

# **FHIR Writes**

**Author:** [Anne Oursler](mailto:aoursler@verily.com)  
**Date:** Jan 29, 2026   
**Team:** Devices  
**Related Epic:** [ONEVERILY-31765](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-31765)  
**Launch:** [https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/LAUNCH-16869](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/LAUNCH-16869)  

| Reviewer | Status | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| [Maria Zakhalyavko](mailto:mariaoz@verily.com) | Completed | LGTM |
| [Arjun Gupta](mailto:guptarju@verily.health) | Completed | LGTM |
| [Jerome Adam-Cote](mailto:jeromeadamcote@verily.health) | Completed | Feb 10, 2026 |
| [Kara Yimoyines](mailto:karayim@verily.com) | Completed | Testing strategy/environment TBD |

### Haiku by [Noah Adler](mailto:ndadler@verily.health):

| *Im not healthkitting When I excitingly say Config saves the day* |
| :---- |

# “What makes this night different from any other night”

In the past, we’ve imported plenty of types of resources from HealthKit and Health Connect. This will be the first time we import a resource that will need a different FHIR mapping based on the contents of that resource from HK/HC. This is because while both CGM and BGM create glucose readings, they use different sources within the body to take those readings, and as such the clinical team will evaluate glucose differently based on the different devices and sources. Differences in the Clinical Team’s views will be discussed in later document tabs. The primary question for this document tab is **“How will we differentiate and treat glucose readings differently based on source”.**

# Links

[FHIR Data Model \- CGM HK/HC](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HHveL03UqFKs2SlchAjuPCbShTPJd4s0AL1dIrNKMJI/edit?tab=t.nwxzypjlzwg0) 

# Overview

This design covers ingesting Glucose data from HealthKit and Health Connect (HK/HC) into the Devices backend and writing it to the FHIR Store. Unlike other biometrics (e.g., Heart Rate Variability), the Glucose proto messages and FHIR mapping infrastructure already exist in the Devices Healthdata Backend. This design focuses on adding HealthKit and Health Connect as new data sources for the existing glucose infrastructure.

Glucose data will be sent from the Mobile apps through the Verily Me App BFF to the Devices Healthdata Backend, where it will be transformed into FHIR Observation resources and stored in the FHIR Store. 

However, existing Philips glucose data is strictly Blood Glucose Monitor (BGM) data, which measures glucose in blood using LOINC code **41653-7**. HealthKit and Health Connect can provide both BGM and Continuous Glucose Monitor (CGM) data. CGMs measure glucose in interstitial fluid rather than blood and record data averaging over a period of time. This in turn means they require a different LOINC code **97507-8** per FHIR/LOINC standards. We will use the deviceType field to distinguish between BGM and  CGM readings, and we will include a list backed by a configuration for the Mobile apps to use to distinguish these devices prior to sending the device system glucose measurements. 

**Out of scope (In another document tab)**

* [Reading Glucose data from FHIR back to Verily Me App](?tab=t.5jscbaq2w5n2)  
* [Volume and performance optimization for high-frequency CGM data]()  
* Displays for CGM data for [Verily Me Web MFE]() and [Enterprise Console MFE]()

# Open Questions

**Can the Mobile devices query for both mmol/L and mg/dL?**

It sounds like not really. The user will be setting their preferred unit when they enter the data, so we’ll need to convert to both formats and be ready to display it in either format.

**HealthKit/Health Connect Contextual Fields** (Optional)

* Does [HK](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/healthkit/hkquantitytypeidentifier/bloodglucose#Metadata-Keys)/HC provide \`reading\_range\` (LOW, TARGET, HIGH, VERY\_HIGH)?  
* Does [HK](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/healthkit/hkquantitytypeidentifier/bloodglucose#Metadata-Keys)/[HC](https://www.loom.com/i/23843dd1e31f41d29b155482d292ff6f) provide \`reading\_qualifier\` (BEFORE\_BREAKFAST, AFTER\_LUNCH, etc.)?

**Clinical Informatics**

- [x] ~~Clinical Informatics must approve the use of LOINC **97507-8** for CGM interstitial fluid glucose readings~~  
- [ ] Clinical Informatics must inspect existing BGM formats for any other fields which should be adjusted for CGM data

# Architecture

![][image2]

# Detailed Design

## Data Flow

Standard biometrics flow (identical to other HK/HC biometrics):

1. Mobile apps retrieve Glucose data from HealthKit (iOS) or Health Connect (Android)  
2. Mobile apps send the data to the Verily Me App BFF via the UpdateConsumerDeviceMeasurement RPC  
3. The Verily Me App BFF forwards this data to the Devices Healthdata Backend via the UpsertBiometricsMeasurements endpoint  
4. The Devices Healthdata Backend transforms the proto message into FHIR Observation resources  
5. The FHIR resources are written to the FHIR Store

## Proto Messages

The \`Glucose\` message already exists in \`device/healthdata/api/v1/biometrics-measurements.proto\` with support for:

* glucose (double) \- The glucose value  
* unit (GlucoseUnit) \- MILLIGRAMS\_PER\_DECILITER or MILLIMOLES\_PER\_LITER  
* reading\_range (ReadingRange) \- LOW, TARGET, HIGH, VERY\_HIGH (optional)  
* reading\_qualifier (ReadingQualifier) \- BEFORE/AFTER meal/activity context (optional)  
* device\_type (DeviceType) \- \*\*BGM or CGM\*\* (critical for LOINC code selection)  
* Time of measurement fore each measurement  
* We also need to ingest Timezone for each measurement

The proto is already part of the \`BiometricsMeasurement.measurement\_type\` oneof and supports both HealthKit and Health Connect aggregators. **For this change we will require the DeviceType as without this we would not be able to correctly map the data source in FHIR as from blood or interstitial fluid.**

## FHIR Mapping

Glucose data will be mapped to FHIR R4 Observation resources following the FHIR Vital Signs profile. The LOINC Code Selection will need to happen based on Device Type (BGM or CGM). The current implementation focuses on BGM data from Phillips, and so uses LOINC **41653-7** "Glucose \[Mass/volume\] in Blood". The new implementation including both Philips and HealthKit/Health Connect with both BGM and CGM support will require the deviceType field in the proto message be populated as either BGM or CGM. 

* BGM data (Blood Glucose Monitor): LOINC **41653-7** "Glucose \[Mass/volume\] in Capillary blood by Glucometer"  
* CGM data (Continuous Glucose Monitor): LOINC **97507-8** "Average glucose \[Mass/volume\] in Interstitial fluid during Reporting Period"  
* Data from an unknown device type will be assumed to be BGM data. If we detect it is actually CGM data we will rely on a Backfill and update the CGM configuration list.

## FHIR Resource Structure

**CGM Observation (Interstitial Fluid Glucose) Example:**

Excerpted from the 97507-8 example in https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/cgm/\#cgm-data-submission-bundles

```json
{
    "resource" : {
        "resourceType" : "Observation",
        "status" : "final",
        "category" : [
          {
            "coding" : [
              {
                "system" : "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category",
                "code" : "laboratory"
              }
            ]
          }
        ],
        "code" : {
          "coding" : [
            {
              "system" : "http://loinc.org",
              "code" : "97507-8"
            }
          ]
        },
        "subject" : {
          🔗 "reference" : "Patient/HKorHCPatient"
        },
        "effectivePeriod" : {
          "start" : "2024-05-01",
          "end" : "2024-05-31"
        },
        "valueQuantity" : {
          "value" : 145,
          "unit" : "mg/dl",
          "system" : "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
          "code" : "mg/dL"
        }
      },
}
```

## Supported CGM Configurations

To support Mobile’s ability to confidently differentiate between BGM and CGM derived glucose readings, we will add and begin maintaining a configured list of the supported CGMs currently available in the US. The [American Association of Clinical Endocrinology currently recognizes 11 devices as CGMs; there](%20https://pro.aace.com/cgm/toolkit/cgm-device-comparison) are a handful more fully implanted CGMs as well.  
They are as follows:

| CGM System | HealthKit/Health Connect Device Name |
| :---- | :---- |
| Abbott FreeStyle Libre 2 | "FreeStyle Libre 2" ​ |
| Abbott FreeStyle Libre 2 Plus | "FreeStyle Libre 2 Plus" ​ |
| Abbott FreeStyle Libre 14 day | "FreeStyle Libre 14 day" |
| Abbott FreeStyle Libre 3 | "FreeStyle Libre 3" ​ |
| Abbott FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus | "FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus" ​ |
| Abbott Lingo | “Lingo” “lingo glucose biosensor” |
| Dexcom G6 Pro | "Dexcom" or "Dexcom G6" |
| Dexcom G6 | "Dexcom" or "Dexcom G6" |
| Dexcom G7 | "Dexcom" or "Dexcom G7" |
| Dexcom Stelo | "Dexcom Stelo" ​ |
| Medtronic Guardian Connect | "Guardian Connect" ​ |
| Medtronic Guardian 3 | "Guardian 3" or "MiniMed" ​ |
| Medtronic Guardian 4 | "Guardian 4" or "MiniMed 780G" ​ |
| Medtronic Simplera | "Simplera" (limited data; likely) ​ |
| Medtronic Instinct | "Instinct" or "MiniMed 780G" (predicted) ​ |
| Eversense 365 CGM System | "Eversense 365" or "Eversense" ​ |
| Eversense E3 | "Eversense" or "Eversense CGM" |

Although we are aware that maintaining a list of CGMs will not be scalable in the long term, it will allow us to move quickly in the short term and meet our short term deadlines. Without this method we will need further research into ways to differentiate CGM and BGM data, and are unlikely to be able to hit our deadlines. Furthermore to support this list of CGMs in the short and mid terms, we will write a backfill to update any CGM Device data which gets initially added as a BGM. That backfill is documented in a later tab, but should pair nicely with an update to the CGM list as a way of adding new devices and fixing any cases of them that we’ve already ingested.

## ListKnownCgmDeviceMeta API

## Finally, to make this configured list available to the mobile team, we will add a new API ListKnownCgmDeviceMeta which will allow the Mobile apps and Mobile BFF to access the configured list of CGMs as needed throughout their processes and enable them to check the strings we expect to see in from HK/HC in the device name fields against this list. Since this config lives in the device system it will be simple to update and backfill far faster than a config within the Mobile apps.

**Protos:**

```textproto
// KnownCgmDeviceMeta represents configurations useful to indetify Glucose readings as relating to a CGM device.
message KnownCgmDeviceMeta {
    // Please Note: HealthConnect also supports specimineSource fields which should be used as a primary before these configs. If the speciminSource field is SPECIMEN_SOURCE_INTERSTITIAL_FLUID = 1 it should be a CGM reading regardless of other fields. 

    // Known Device Models
    repeated string device_model = 0;

    // Device manufacters who only manufactor CGM Devices
    repeated string device_manufacturer = 1;

    // User Facing Device Names of supported devices 
    repeated string device_name = 2; and a name
}
```

See [HealthKit](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/healthkit/hkdevice) and [Health Connect](https://developer.android.com/reference/androidx/health/connect/client/records/metadata/Device#Device\(kotlin.Int,kotlin.String,kotlin.String\)) Device Definitions for more details on the information we receive about devices within a measurement.

**Per Environment Yaml Configurations:**

```xml
  supportedCGMConfig:                                                        
    # Dexcom G7                                                            
    - device_model:
        - "G7"
        - "Dexcom G7"
    - device_manufacturer:                                                 
        - "Dexcom"                                                         
        - "Dexcom, Inc."                                                   
    - device_name:                                                         
        - "Dexcom G7"
        - "G7 CGM"

    # Dexcom Stelo
    - device_model:
        - "Stelo"
        - "Dexcom Stelo"
    - device_manufacturer:
        - "Dexcom"
        - "Dexcom, Inc."
    - device_name:
        - "Stelo"
        - "Stelo Glucose Biosensor"
        - "Dexcom Stelo"

    # Abbott FreeStyle Libre 3
    - device_model:
        - "FreeStyle Libre 3"
        - "Libre 3"
        - "72081-01"  # UDI model number
        - "72080-01"  # UDI model number
    - device_manufacturer: # Empty as Abbott also manufactures BGMs
    - device_name:
        - "FreeStyle Libre 3"
        - "Libre 3"
        - "FreeStyle Libre 3 Sensor"
```

**Endpoint Protos:**

```textproto
// Get the list of supported CGM devices and their platform identifiers
rpc ListKnownCgmDeviceMeta(ListKnownCgmDeviceMetaRequest) returns
  (ListKnownCgmDeviceMetaResponse) {
      option (ciam.annotations.permissions) = {
        ciam: ["ALLOW_AUTHENTICATED"],
      };
}

// Request message for ListKnownCgmDeviceMeta
message ListKnownCgmDeviceMetaRequest {
// Empty - no parameters needed, returns all supported devices
}

// Response message for ListKnownCgmDeviceMeta
message ListKnownCgmDeviceMetaResponse {
    // The complete CGM device configuration
    repeated SupportedCGMConfig config = 1;
}
```

# Implementation Notes

We’ll add new constants for the new LOINC Code: **97507-8** and **Average glucose \[Mass/volume\] in Interstitial fluid during Reporting Period**. We’ll use these new constants to set Glucose Specific fields, differentiation based on the device type sent to us by Mobile. We will also update the query logic currently located in the Device Healthdata Service to support both CGM and BGM LOINC Codes when querying glucose observations.

# Requirements Mapping

This work is covered by existing requirements for biometrics ingestion:

* REQ-1343e4cf "Upsert Biometrics Observations" in \`device/docs/requirements/01-healthdata/biometrics-observations/biometrics-observations.yaml\`

Glucose is already a supported biometric measurement type, and no new requirements should be needed. The existing acceptance criteria were designed to cover the glucose ingestion from multiple sources

# Testing

We will follow existing Device system best practices for testing, including unit tests as needed, and integration tests to cover the new CGM LOINC Code paths and differentiation between the device types for FHIR mapping. This will ensure we test the entire end to end flow within the Device System.

Integration tests should cover:

* End-to-end CGM glucose from HK/HC  
* End-to-end BGM glucose from HK/HC  
* Unknown device\_type defaulting to BGM with a warning  
* Verify both LOINC codes are queryable

# Feature Flag

The flag \`FEATURE\_GLUCOSE\_HEALTHKIT\_HEALTHCONNECT\_ENABLED\` will be added to control the ingestion of glucose data from HK/HC until it is complete and ready for launch. Without it requests to upsert Glucose data will be rejected.

# Task Breakdown

**Epic:** [ONEVERILY-31765](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-31765)

| Task | Points(by complexity) |
| :---- | :---- |
| Coordinate with Mobile team on DeviceType detection strategy and Config | 2 |
| Define SupportedCGMConfig proto messages with HealthKitFields and HealthConnectFields  | 2 |
| Set up empty / stubbed yaml for CGM Devices Config | 1 |
| Set up endpoint for ListSupportedCGMsConfig | 3 |
| Integration test ListSupportedCGMsConfig  | 3 |
| Populate SupportedCGMsConfig | ??? |
| Coordinate with Clinical Informatics to define new CGM Observation Profile and example |  |
| Update Cortex data contract configs if required for new CGM Observation Profile | 1 |
| Add feature flag FEATURE\_GLUCOSE\_HEALTHKIT\_HEALTHCONNECT\_ENABLED | 1 |
| Add LOINC constants for CGM (**97507-8**) in biometrics.go | 1 |
| Modify Device Healthdata system to differentiate based on LOINC Codes. | 3 |
| Integration tests for HealthKit/Health Connect glucose flow  | 3 |
| Manual End to End Test with the Mobile team | 3 |

# Privacy Considerations

Glucose data is already covered under the existing PIA. The PIA was written to be general enough to cover biometric vital signs data, including glucose from HealthKit and Health Connect.

# Security Considerations

This feature follows existing patterns for biometric data ingestion. There are no new security considerations beyond the existing security measures.

# Alternatives Considered

We don’t currently have a seamless way to determine if measurements from HK and HC are from CGMs or BGMs, and the area of responsibility for this determination is not yet fully clear. Although we have selected the method of passing a config to Mobile, and having them make the determination, we did consider a number of other options briefly discussed below:

**Write data without a device type, and use an enrichment to set frequent and regular observations to CGM and irregular observations to BGM**  
This option has been discarded as we do not want to write bad data to FHIR, and a glucose observation for which we cannot determine if it is considered BGM or CGM data is bad data.

**Have the Device System determine responsibility for the device type**  
As the Device system has even less data the the Mobile app this option has been discarded

**Hardcode a list of available CGM devices into the Mobile Apps**  
Hardcoding a list of available CGMs into the Mobile App may be the simplest option, but it means that updating the list when a new CGM becomes available would require a release of the Mobile apps, and thus be a longer and more drawn out process.

**\[Accepted\] Forward a configured list of CGM devices to the Mobile Apps, which will determine the device type**  
 By passing a config for the mobile apps to use in their determination we can ensure that the decision is made using as much information as is available, and that the decision is made as soon as the data enters the OneVerily ecosystem. The Device System can gate glucose readings, ensuring that glucose readings are never written to FHIR without the correct LOINC code and that unknown device readings throw an error rather than writing bad data.

# 📄 CGM Data Backfill

# **Continuous Glucose Monitoring Glucose Data Backfill**

**Author:** [Anne Oursler](mailto:aoursler@verily.com)  
**Date:** Feb 6, 2026   
**Team:** Devices  
**Related Epic:** None Needed  
**Launch:** None Needed

| Reviewer | Status | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| [Maria Zakhalyavko](mailto:mariaoz@verily.com) | Completed | LGTM |
| [Arjun Gupta](mailto:guptarju@verily.health) | Not Started |  |
| [Kara Yimoyines](mailto:karayim@verily.com) | Completed | LGTM |

### Haiku by Gemini:

| *Fetch by device name, Update FHIR as config shifts, Keep the data whole.* |
| :---- |

**Background**  
Given the config based approach described for differentiation CGM and BGM writes, it is somewhat inevitable that we will eventually write a glucose reading from a CGM to the FHIR store as a BGM glucose. To remedy this, we will be able to add the new CGM type to the configured list of CGMs, and then we will need to update the data that was written under that name.

**Overview**  
To prepare for the future, we will write a backfill which is able to fetch glucose readings by a device name, and update the glucose to the CGM format laid about above in FHIR Writes. We will test this backfill in dev-stable, and should not need to turn it on unless we are updating the config list. When we update the config list we should strongly consider running the backfill alongside it, to ensure that any data that was added in advance of the change is also updated.

**Open Questions:**  
Do we have a way to monitor if we need a backfill?  
Do we want an enrichment to identify CGM data

# Task Breakdown

**Epic:** [ONEVERILY-31765](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-31765) (Writes Backfill)

| Task | Points(by complexity) |
| :---- | :---- |
| Stub out and wire in a new Backfill with a feature flag | 2 |
| Query for Observations with either CGM or BGM Loinc and a specific deviceName | 3 |
| Convert and update BGM or CGM for a specific deviceName | 3 |
| Unit test new Backfill | 2 |
| Test Backfill in Dev Stable | 2 |
| Turn Backfill on everywhere | 1 |

# Filter on \_lastUpdated in listBiometrics

See ask from mobile: [https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-106988](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-106988)

# Overview

Problem: Enable the mobile team to easily access larger biometric data sets in anticipation of CGM

* Currently mobile can only request data based on effectiveTime, biometric data type, and participantId  
* With cgm these levers become less useful, as there will be a high concentration of data even at that scale  
* Ex: Imagine a data set of size 1000  
  * Assume that mobile queried/processed it yesterday  
  * But now today there is one more data point which mobile needs to process  
  * Today they need to request the entire data set. Again…

Solution: Enable biometric data to be queried in digestible and meaningful chunks

* \_lastUpdated field is very reliable  
* Using \_lastUpdated mobile can page through cgm data in chunks, starting from oldest records to newest records

Example of how mobile might use this solution:

1) Remember some timestamp, initialize to 1/1/1970 if this is the first iteration  
   1) Query for data  
   2) Process data  
   3) Remember latest timestamp from data  
   4) \[Every X Interval\] Go back to step 1 w/ timestamp from step above

Solution overview:

* [Fix Pagination](#fixing-pagination)  
* [Filtering/Sorting on \_lastUpdated](#filtering/sorting-on-_lastupdated-as-a-theory:)  
* [Introduce \`updatedSince\` field into ListBiometricMeasurements request](#introduce-\`lastupdated\`-field-into-listbiometricmeasurements-request:)  
* [Implement filter and sort](#filtering/sorting-on-_lastupdated-as-a-theory:)  
* [Expose \`lastUpdated\`](#expose-\`lastupdated\`-on-biometric-measurements)  
* [Deduplication](#deduplication-of-existing-measurement-updates)

# Deep Dive into Solution:

## **Fixing pagination** {#fixing-pagination}

We never got around to implementing pagination, so let's get around to it now

FHIR Pagination: [https://docs.cloud.google.com/healthcare-api/docs/how-tos/best-practices-fhir-search](https://docs.cloud.google.com/healthcare-api/docs/how-tos/best-practices-fhir-search)

## **Filtering/Sorting on \_lastUpdated as a theory:** {#filtering/sorting-on-_lastupdated-as-a-theory:}

* Automatically indexed by FHIR stores  
* Already used in production queries throughout the codebase  
* Supports prefix modifiers like ge, gt, lt, le for range queries  
* Its already used in backfill job code as well as fetchOrganization code

Oliver: 

* “page tokens are opaque but expected to follow the sort order under no mutations”  
* “if you want all of the data it may be more suitable to use oldest \--\> newest update and that guarantees at least once inclusion of each record”

```protobuf
// add these into the ListBiometricMeasurementsRequest so users can sort by e

  Column sorted_by = 7;
  enum Column {
    COLUMN_UNSPECIFIED = 0;
    COLUMN_EFFECTIVE_TIMESTAMP = 1;
    COLUMN_LAST_UPDATES = 2
  }

  Order sort_order = 8;
  enum Order {
    ORDER_UNSPECIFIED = 0;
    ORDER_ASCENDING = 1;
    ORDER_DESCENDING = 2;
  }

```

## **Introduce \`lastUpdated\` field into ListBiometricMeasurements request:** {#introduce-`lastupdated`-field-into-listbiometricmeasurements-request:}

* See proposed change below  
* Mobile will use \`updatedSince\` to get recently updated measurements

```go
message ListBiometricsMeasurementsRequest {
    repeated string patient_ids = 1;
    MeasurementType measurement_type = 2;

    // If unspecified, the default is 50.
    // The max value is 1000 (FHIR limitation for MaxPageSize).
    int32 page_size = 6;
    string page_token = 7;

+   // ------------- NEW ------------------------ 
+   optional google.protobuf.Timestamp updated_since = 8 
+   // ------------- NEW (end) ------------------
}
```

## **Return measurements after \`req.updatedSince\`**

* We can use filtering in FHIR [here](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/33a99ea3acdd948755a7003e7d3696605dcf1e0f/device/healthdata/internal/storage/fhir_observation_store_biometrics.go#L234) to return measurements after a given date, see code below

```go
// Filter by _lastUpdated if UpdatedSince is provided
if opts.UpdatedSince != nil {
    params = append(params, &gcp.FhirQueryParameter{
        Key:   "_lastUpdated",
        Value: fmt.Sprintf("ge%s", opts.UpdatedSince.AsTime().Format(time.RFC3339)),
    })
}
```

## **Expose \`lastUpdated\` on biometric measurements** {#expose-`lastupdated`-on-biometric-measurements}

* Mobile must have access to \`lastUpdated\` for any given measurement, so that they know where the last page left off, and how to craft the next page request

```protobuf
message BiometricsMeasurement {
  string aggregator_record_id = 1;
  AggregatorName aggregator_name = 2;
  string patient_id = 3;
  string org_id = 4;
  string origin_name = 5;
  optional string device_manufacturer = 13;
  optional string device_model = 14;
  uint32 record_version = 7;
  string source_record_id = 15;
  string code_provenance = 16;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp start_time = 8;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp end_time = 9;
  oneof measurement_type {
    Steps step = 10;
    HeartRate heart_rate = 11;
    RestingHeartRate resting_heart_rate = 12;
    CompositeBloodPressure composite_blood_pressure = 20;
    Weight weight = 21;
    Glucose glucose = 22;
  }
  optional string e_tag = 17;
  bool is_aggregated = 18;

+   // ------------- NEW ------------------------ 
+    google.protobuf.Timestamp last_updated = 23
+   // ------------- NEW (end) ------------------

}

```

## **Deduplication of existing measurement updates** {#deduplication-of-existing-measurement-updates}

The key to preventing duplicate observations when a data point is updated and re-processed is to use a unique identifier for each measurement record that is consistent across updates.

# Work Estimates:

* \[Protos\]   
  * \[2pt\] Add \`lastUpdated\`, \`sortBy\` and \`sortOrder\` to BE ListBiometricMeasurementsRequest proto  
  * \[1pts\] expose and populate \`lastUpdated\` on biometric measurements  
* \[BE\]  
  * \[2pts\] Support sorting by lastUpdated, ensure a safe-harbor default  
  * \[2pts\] Query for observations whos \`lastUpdated\` is gt \`updatedSince\`  
  * \[3pts\] Support page token for subsequent pages  
* \[Integration tests\]  
  * \[3pt\] Add BE integration tests for lastUpdated, sorting and pagination  
* Total: 10pts  
  * 1 eng: 13 business days (1.25 sprint)  
  * 2 eng: 8 business days (0.75 sprint, or saving about a week)

 

# Open questions

* Do we expect the need to handle other big data sets aside from glucose? \- yes  
* Clarify with Mobile if it is acceptable to to sort Measurements by lastUpdated if this filter is used? [Wendy Chu](mailto:wendychu@verily.health)  
  * Opting not to do this, it's not a requirement nor is it expected. [Wendy Chu](mailto:wendychu@verily.health), feel free to chime in here  
  * The idea is that if those optional args are not provided, then default to same behavior today. i.e. clients who this today wont need to concern themselves with this initiative as a whole (without breaking anything) (safe harbor type strategy)  
  *  enum Order {  
    * ORDER\_UNSPECIFIED \= 0;  
    * ORDER\_ASCENDING \= 1;  
    * ORDER\_DESCENDING \= 2;  
  * }

# Alternatives Considered:

### **Websocket event bus for recently updated measurements**

Overview:

* As new observations come in, publish a message onto a websocket event bus that   
* Setup one message queue per customer, clients mobile device subscribes and consumes  
* Then mobile clients can consume messages:  
  * At their own pace, and at any time after the mobile device is turned on and has internet connection  
  * Provides Realtime updates without a need for any kind of polling (long or short)  
  * Guaranteed at least once delivery (and then mobile would dedupe with IDs to protect against duplicate deliveries)

Limitations:

* Websockets are expensive  
* Not aware of any pattern within verily of this

### **Full Sync with Local Dedup (not feasable)**

Overview::

* Fetch all data every time (no updated\_since)  
* Client deduplicates against local DB using aggregator\_record\_id

Limitations:

* Cannot fetch more than 1000 records, so there is no way to access "all records"

### **Leverage monotonically increasing IDs**

Overview:

* IF we generated monotonically increasing IDs then we could actually use operations like \`gt\` on ids and capture oldest to newest id

Limitations:

* We don't generate monotonically increasing ids, instead they are completely random  
* Also this would not support updates unless we were using the some sort of monotonic id generation with revisions

### 

### **Use a pub-sub event-driven model for mobile to capture all updates**

* This would be the most flexible solution as mobile could consume at their own pace with a guarantee of no data loss,   
* but I believe they want to store data locally on the users mobile phone which is not a good match for this solution.

# 📄 Enterprise Console MFE

# **Continuous Glucose Monitoring Enterprise Console MFE**

**Author:** [Anne Oursler](mailto:aoursler@verily.com)&[Jacob Komissar](mailto:jacob@verily.com)  
**Date:** Jan 29, 2026   
**Team:** Devices  
**Related Epic:** [ONEVERILY-108731](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-108731)

| :---- | :---- | :---- |

| [Arjun Gupta](mailto:guptarju@verily.health) | Not Started |  |
| [Jerome Adam-Cote](mailto:jeromeadamcote@verily.health) | Completed | Waiting on comments to be resolved |
| [Kara Yimoyines](mailto:karayim@verily.com) | Completed | Open questions obviously, but otherwise approved |
| [Yudian Xu](mailto:yudianx@tvc.verily.health) | Completed | LGTM, left a few comments for minor updates. |

### Haiku by [Noah Adler](mailto:ndadler@verily.health):

| *Is this data for CGM or BGM Zoinks\! We shall use Loinc\!* |
| :---- |

# Related Links

[PRD](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1irUQBI7kQjkMNzj_PXlTxboEzUIj4p_6oZ5Pao9w7G8/edit?usp=sharing)

[UX Figmas](https://www.figma.com/design/i35qzUaxiThSJa4tymxN6n/Console-UX-Design-Spec?node-id=13551-751384&t=UaCxVMJ9Kc5YlUYq-4)

# Open Questions:

**Q:** What LOINC Code should we use for CGM data?  
**A: 97507-8** "Average glucose \[Mass/volume\] in Interstitial fluid during Reporting Period"

# Overview

This design covers the work needed to add support for the display of Continuous Glucose Monitor (CGM) data alongside existing Blood Glucose Meter (BGM) data in the professional-facing glucose dashboard. CGM will use LOINC code **97507-8** and will be visualized as a dotted line chart rather than a scatter plot.

## Decisions Summary

**Default View:** The toggle shall default to CGM if the user has a CGM (regardless of BGM status) and to BGM if the user only has a BGM.)

**Toggle View:** Separate charts with toggle switch, which should be visible on both the card and expanded view, even if toggling will result in the “No data” state.

**Timespans:** CGM only supports day and week views. Day view will show a single continuous line for the selected 24 hours. Week view will show 7 separate colored lines (one per day) overlaid on the same 24-hour X-axis. The legend at the bottom of the graph will show these labels. Month view is deliberately not supported.

**Tooltips:**  

Summary view

* Daily chart, show date, min/max of the day  
* Weekly chart, show date min min/max of the day

Expanded view

* When hovering over the line in the daily expanded view,  the tooltip shall display the value at that point/line with the date and time  
* When hovering over a line in an expanded weekly view, we should highlight the line and show the date and the max and min of the highlighted day in a tooltip. 

**Server Side Data Filtering:** Data filtering will take place by device type and result in different FHIR Queries for BGM or CGM data.

**Caching:** The device system will need to set up a Cortex cache for CGM data by selected date range to reduce re-querying Cortex for CGM data.

Date and timezone:

* A day starts and ends when the date changes \- midnight to midnight. (not 24h from present moment)  
* For now we only have time in UTC coming from the app. We should use the browser location to determine the offset/timezone and display accordingly. The “participant timezone” will come as a fast follow.

# Architecture 

User actions in the Professional MFE trigger requests to the Professional BFF. The BFF verifies the needed auth, and makes requests to the Device Backends. The Device Healthdata Backend will be updated to accommodate differentiating CGM and BGM requests based on LOINC Code. The Healthdata service will respond with the needed information from the Operational FHIR Store and enable the Device Glucose Professional MFE to display the appropriate visualization.  
![][image3]

# Implementation

## Healthdata Backend

[device/healthdata/api/v1/biometrics-measurements.proto](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/healthdata/api/v1/biometrics-measurements.proto)  
Add glucose to measurement type enum:

```textproto
enum MeasurementType {
  option allow_alias = true;
  ...
  MEASUREMENT_TYPE_GLUCOSE = 6;  // same as MEASUREMENT_TYPE_GLUCOSE_BGM
  MEASUREMENT_TYPE_GLUCOSE_BGM = 6;
  MEASUREMENT_TYPE_GLUCOSE_CGM = 7;
}
```

[device/healthdata/internal/server/healthdata\_service.go](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/healthdata/internal/server/healthdata_service.go)  
Add \`LoincGlucoseCGM \= "**97507-8**"\` constant and add it to the \`validLoincFilters\` map.

Additionally to support the MFE, we should add an “aggregator” which calculates summary statistics for CGM data on the order of days, noting the high and low values for the CGM data across a day, for each day queried.

## Professional BFF

[device/biometrics/prof/bff/api/v1/glucose\_professional\_service.proto](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/prof/bff/api/v1/glucose_professional_service.proto)  
Add new CGM data request message and CGM endpoint path mirroring that of the BGM endpoint

```textproto
message GetGlucoseCgmCardDataRequest {
  // Professional Account making this request.
  // Format: "professionals/{fhir_practitioner_id}".
  string professional = 1 [
    (ciam.annotations.inputs) = {is_actor: true},
    (google.api.resource_reference) = {type: "verily.health/Professional"},
    (verily.privacy.annotations.scrub) = false,
    (google.api.field_behavior) = REQUIRED
  ];

  // Participant Record to fetch Glucose card data on.
  // Format: "participantRecords/{fhir_patient_id}".
  string participant_record = 2 [
    (ciam.annotations.inputs) = {is_target: true},
    (google.api.resource_reference) = {type: "verily.health/ParticipantRecord"},
    (verily.privacy.annotations.scrub) = false,
    (google.api.field_behavior) = REQUIRED
  ];

  // Start time of request
  optional google.protobuf.Timestamp start_time = 3;
  // End time of request
  optional google.protobuf.Timestamp end_time = 4;
  // Maximum records to return
  optional uint32 max_records = 5;
}
```

[device/biometrics/prof/bff/internal/service/glucose/glucose\_professional.go](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/prof/bff/internal/service/glucose/glucose_professional.go)

## Professional MFE

### Card Layout

A new toggle switch will be added to the Glucose card’s header row to switch between BGM and CGM modes.

### ![][image4]

[device/biometrics/prof/mfe/src/cards/glucose/components/GlucoseGraphContainer.tsx](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/prof/mfe/src/cards/glucose/components/GlucoseGraphContainer.tsx)  
Note: ToggleButton exists in RDS, but we may need a different component to achieve the correct design.

Currently, the header of each of the cards is configured solely by the card’s title; we will need to add an extension point to allow inserting a switch and passing its state to the rest of the card’s components. A little architecture work will be needed to ensure 

The basic idea might look something like this:

1. Export \`GlucoseViewType \= 'bgm' | 'cgm'\` type  
2. Add \`viewType\` property set by the switch and passed to the other components by context.  
3. Ensure that changes to \`viewType\` correctly update the card’s state:  
   1. Use conditional rendering to switch the entire chart for a new CGM line chart component.  
   2. Use state management or an alternative setup mechanism to switch from month to week view when entering CGM mode.

### Line Chart

### ![][image5]

[device/biometrics/prof/mfe/src/cards/glucose/components/GlucoseLineChart/GlucoseLineChart.tsx](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/prof/mfe/src/cards/glucose/components/GlucoseLineChart/GlucoseLineChart.tsx)

CGM-specific adaptations  
Chart configuration:

* Add a configurable \`ReferenceArea\` to support CGM 70-180 mg/dL range  
* Ensure the line can be drawn as data points only, without an actual line through them

Day view:

* Transform measurements to chronologically sorted line data (timestamp, glucose value)

Week view:

* Group measurements by day of week (Sun-Sat)  
* Normalize timestamps to time-of-day (0-24 hours) for X-axis overlay  
* Render 7 separate lines with different colors  
* Each line shows one day's pattern overlaid on same 24-hour timeline  
* Include a legend to show day labels

### ToolTips

Tool tips will differ depending on the duration of data in focus. For Daily expanded view data, we will display the date and glucose at the point of the tooltip, snapping to the nearest reading to where the member clicks or hovers.  
![][image6]  
For Weekly views, we will highlight the selected line, letting the other lines fade to grey. From there the tooltip will display the date and stats about the entire line representing the full day’s data.  
![][image7]

### Expected Range

1. Update \`isGlucoseInRange()\` to accept \`deviceType\` parameter and use range 70-180 mg/dL  
2. Update \`computeGlucoseSummaryStats()\` to accept \`deviceType\` parameter and pass it to \`isGlucoseInRange()\` when calculating in-range count

### Interactive Legend

[Jacob Komissar](mailto:jacob@verily.com)to fill in ([reference](https://recharts.github.io/en-US/examples/LegendEffectOpacity/))  
When we click on the legend for each day of the week, we would like to highlight the associated line, for  example clicking on the monday line would result in the graph above.

### Empty State Handling

In the GlucoseGraphContainer, add conditional rendering: when \`glucoseMeasurements\` is empty, show centered "No data" message with device type label; otherwise show appropriate chart. Toggle should remain visible in both states.

# Testing Strategy

Unit tests should be written for each code change per best practice. Thus this section will focus on the expected updates to integration testing:

## Healthdata Backend

Verify that we can

* Create FHIR Observation with CGM LOINC code (97507-8)  
* Verify ListObservations can return CGM observations  
* Set up a test patient with both BGM and CGM data

## Biometrics Prof BFF

Ensure that 

* Mock healthdata responses can have mixed observations  
* GetGlucoseCardData with device\_type=BGM only returns BGM observations  
* GetGlucoseCardData with device\_type=CGM only returns CGM observations  
* GetGlucoseCardData without device\_type returns both BGM and CGM

## Biometrics Prof MFE

Add tests that

* Toggle between BGM/CGM views  
* Verify correct chart type renders for each view  
* Verify stats update correctly per device type

# Task Breakdown

**Epic:** [ONEVERILY-108731](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-108731)

| Task | Points(by complexity) |
| :---- | :---- |
| Update healthdata proto and endpoint with CGM device type | 3 |
| Add CGM data aggregator for daily statistics | 3 |
| Add healthdata integration tests for CGM | 2 |
| Update BFF proto and endpoint with device\_type filter  | 3 |
| Add CGM LOINC and helper methods to BFF service | 3 |
| Update BFF observation fetching to use device type filtering | 3 |
| Add BFF integration tests for device type filtering | 2 |
| Add feature flag to gate new CGM display on the MFE Feature Flag |  |
| Put together skeleton MFE (with dummy components) | 1 |
| Implement mock data generation for CGM | 2? |
| Add field in console MFE structure to slot in BGM/CGM toggle |  |
| Add BGM/CGM toggle to Glucose card | ? |
| Create GlucoseLineChart component with day and week views |  |
| Implement GlucoseLineChart week view with multiple colored lines per day and appropriate hover behavior on the lines and legend |  |
| Update expected range logic for CGM vs BGM device types |  |
| Handle empty states in GlucoseGraphContainer |  |
| Add MFE unit tests for CGM functionality |  |
| Research a reasonable TTL for observations | 2 |
| Use Cortex FHIR Proxy cache to support a “most recent measurements cache” | 5 |
| Use Cortex FHIR Proxy cache to support search time-range query style caching | 5 |
| Add integration testing verifying cache and TTL | 3 |
| Watch cache roll out to prod in dashboard latency metrics and alerts | 2 |

# 

# ✅ Reference: CGM vs BGM

# CGM vs BGM Data Comparison

| Feature | BGM (Blood Glucose Meter) | CGM (Continuous Glucose Monitor) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| LOINC Codes | 41653-7, 41652-9 | 97507-8 |
| Reading Frequency | 2-10 readings/day | 100-300 readings/day (every 5-15 min) |
| Typical Data Volume (7 days) | \~50 records | \~2,100 records |
| Visualization | Scatter chart (dots) | Line chart (continuous) |
| Reference Range | 80-180 mg/dL | 70-180 mg/dL |
| Chart Components | GlucoseScatterChart (existing) | GlucoseLineChart (new) |
| Supported Timespans | Day, Week, Month | Day, Week |
| Week View | All data on single timeline | 7 separate lines (one per day overlaid) |

# CGM vs BGM LOINC Codes

BGM (Blood Glucose Meter):

*   41653-7: Glucose \[Mass/volume\] in Capillary blood by Glucometer  
*   41652-9: Glucose \[Moles/volume\] in Capillary blood by Glucometer

CGM (Continuous Glucose Monitor):

*   97507-8: Glucose \[Mass/volume\] in Blood from CGM

# ✅ RDS Oportunities

# RDS (React Design System)

#  Reusability Opportunities

This document identifies areas where the React Design System (RDS) can be leveraged for better code reuse and consistency across patient and professional MFEs. We should consider in our implementation if we can save time and pain in the future by investing in the use and re-use of RDS components.

# Existing RDS Components (Use)

**ToggleButtonGroup / ToggleButton:** Standard MUI components that should be imported from RDS which we should use in GlucoseGraphContainer.tsx for the BGM/CGM view toggle.  
**TimeRangeExplorer:** Exists in RDS and should require minimal changes to be useful.  
Typography, Box: Standard MUI layout components which should be imported from RDS for consistency for use in empty state messages and stat card titles.

# New RDS Components (Potentially Add)

**LoadingOverlay:** The Loading overlay may not yet exist in RDS, but especially with higher volume changes like CGM data we expect it will become more important.   
Line Chart: We will need to create a \`GlucoseLineChart.tsx\` in professional MFE. We expect this will not be the last time we need a line chart. For example \`WeightLineChart.tsx\` already exists and we could reuse the component.   
**Utility Functions:**

* Time-of-Day Normalization (Week View) to ensure consistent week view behavior across all biometrics within the dashboard.  
* Reference Range Utilities as calling out in and out of range data seems more and more likely to be a part of our MFEs.

# ❌ Verily Me Web MFE

# **\[DEFERED/DEPRECATED\]**

# **Continuous Glucose Monitoring VerilyMe Web MFE**

**Author:** [Anne Oursler](mailto:aoursler@verily.com)  
**Date:** Jan 29, 2026   
**Team:** Devices  
**Related Epic:  [ONEVERILY-109757: \[Device\] Create CGM MFE for VMe Web](https://verily.atlassian.net/browse/ONEVERILY-109757)**

| Reviewer | Status | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| [Maria Zakhalyavko](mailto:mariaoz@verily.com) | Not Started |  |
| [Arjun Gupta](mailto:guptarju@verily.health) | Not Started |  |
| [Jerome Adam-Cote](mailto:jeromeadamcote@verily.health) | Not Started |  |
| [Kara Yimoyines](mailto:karayim@verily.com) | Not Started |  |

# Open Questions

Are there UX Figmas for the patient-facing CGM implementation? [https://www.figma.com/design/YkPXT6lBzktBzMTCSf8fRG/%F0%9F%8F%80-Dashboard\_Playground?node-id=4016-168075\&m=dev](https://www.figma.com/design/YkPXT6lBzktBzMTCSf8fRG/%F0%9F%8F%80-Dashboard_Playground?node-id=4016-168075&m=dev)

Do we still intend to update to a Candlesticks Graph? Just in VerilyMe?

# Overview

This design covers the work needed to add support for the display of Continuous Glucose Monitor (CGM) data alongside existing Blood Glucose Meter (BGM) data in the patient-facing glucose dashboard. CGM will use LOINC code **97507-8** and will be visualized as a line chart rather than a scatter plot.

## Decisions Summary

**Default View:** By default we will show BGM data, and fallback to CGM data if BGM data is empty

* If patient has BGM data → show BGM  
* If patient has no BGM but has CGM → show CGM  
* If patient has both → show BGM (user can toggle)


**Toggle View:** Separate charts with toggle switch, which should be visible on both the landing card and details view, even if toggling will result in the "No data" state.

**Reference Ranges:** CGM reference ranges differ from BGM ranges, and should be 70-180 mg/dL (vs BGM 80-180 mg/dL)

**Timespans:** CGM only supports day and week views. Day view will show a single continuous line for the selected 24 hours. Week view will show 7 separate colored lines (one per day) overlaid on the same 24-hour X-axis. Month view is deliberately not supported.

**Server Side Data Filtering:** Data filtering will take place by device type and result in different FHIR Queries for BGM or CGM data via LOINC code.

**Caching:** The device system will need to set up a Cortex cache for CGM data by selected date range to reduce re-querying Cortex for CGM data.

# Architecture

User actions in the VerilyMe Web within the Device MFE trigger requests to the Patient BFF. The BFF verifies the needed auth and consents, and makes requests to the Device Backends. The Device Healthdata Backend will be updated to accommodate differentiating CGM and BGM requests based on LOINC Code. The Healthdata service will respond with the needed information from the Operational FHIR Store and enable the Device Glucose Patient MFE to display the appropriate visualization.  
![][image8]

# Implementation

## Healthdata Backend

[device/healthdata/api/v1/biometrics-measurements.proto](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/healthdata/api/v1/biometrics-measurements.proto)  
Add CGM to DeviceType enum:

```textproto
enum DeviceType {
  DEVICE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED = 0;
  BGM = 1;
  CGM = 2;
}
```

[device/healthdata/internal/server/healthdata\_service.go](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/healthdata/internal/server/healthdata_service.go)  
Add LoincGlucoseCGM \= "**97507-8**" constant and add it to the validLoincFilters map.

## Patient BFF

[device/biometrics/patient/bff/api/v1/glucose\_service.proto](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/patient/bff/api/v1/glucose_service.proto)  
Add optional device\_type filter to both request messages:

```textproto
message ListGlucoseMeasurementsRequest {
  ...
  optional DeviceType device_type = 3;
}

message ListAggregatedGlucoseMeasurementsRequest {
  ...
  optional DeviceType device_type = 5;
}
```

[device/biometrics/patient/bff/internal/service/glucose/glucose\_patient.go](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/patient/bff/internal/service/glucose/glucose_patient.go)

1. Add LoincGlucoseCGMCode \= "**97507-8**" constant   
2. Add getLoincCodesForDeviceType(deviceType) helper function that returns appropriate LOINC codes based on device type and determineDeviceType(fhirObservation) method that examines LOINC codes in observation to return appropriate DeviceType.  
3. Update hard coded BGM cases to use helper functions and route deviceType.

## Patient MFE

### Common Utils

[device/biometrics/patient/mfe/src/common/glucoseUtils.ts](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/patient/mfe/src/common/glucoseUtils.ts)  
Export device-specific reference range constants:

```textproto
export const BGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MIN = 80;
export const BGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MAX = 180;
export const CGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MIN = 70;
export const CGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MAX = 180;
```

// Update existing constants to point to BGM values for backward compatibility

```textproto
export const GLUCOSE_REFERENCE_RANGE_MIN = BGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MIN;
export const GLUCOSE_REFERENCE_RANGE_MAX = BGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MAX;
```

Update isGlucoseInRange() to accept optional deviceType parameter and use device-specific ranges:

```textproto
export const isGlucoseInRange = (
  reading: number,
  deviceType?: DeviceType
): boolean => {
  const min = deviceType === DeviceType.CGM ? CGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MIN : BGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MIN;
  const max = deviceType === DeviceType.CGM ? CGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MAX : BGM_REFERENCE_RANGE_MAX;
  return reading >= min && reading <= max;
};
```

### Landing Card

[device/biometrics/patient/mfe/src/components/GlucoseLandingCard/GlucoseLandingCard.tsx](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/patient/mfe/src/components/GlucoseLandingCard/GlucoseLandingCard.tsx)

* Add state for viewType: 'bgm' | 'cgm' and hasMultipleDeviceTypes: boolean  
  * On initial data load, determine if both BGM and CGM data exist  
  * Set default viewType based on data availability (BGM if present, else CGM)  
* Add ToggleButtonGroup (from RDS) with BGM/CGM options if hasMultipleDeviceTypes is true  
* Update data fetching calls to include device\_type parameter based on current viewType  
* Show AggregatedGlucoseScatterChart for BGM, GlucoseLineChart for CGM  
* Show toggle even when data would be empty for one view type

### Details View

[device/biometrics/patient/mfe/src/components/DetailsView/DetailsView.tsx](https://github.com/verily-src/verily1/blob/main/device/biometrics/patient/mfe/src/components/DetailsView/DetailsView.tsx)

#### Graph Container

* Add state for viewType: 'bgm' | 'cgm' and hasMultipleDeviceTypes: boolean  
* Add ToggleButtonGroup with BGM/CGM options above the TimeRangeExplorer (visible if hasMultipleDeviceTypes)  
* Filter timeRangeOptions to exclude MONTH when viewType \=== 'cgm'  
* Add useEffect to auto-switch from month to week if user toggles to CGM while on month view  
* Update data loading calls to include device\_type parameter  
* Replace chart rendering with conditional: render AggregatedGlucoseScatterChart for BGM, GlucoseLineChart for CGM

#### Line Chart

Create a new GlucoseLineChart which uses existing line charts and also supports CGM-specific adaptations like the following:  
Chart configuration with configurable ReferenceArea for 70-180 mg/dL range  
Day view:

* Transform measurements to chronologically sorted line data (timestamp, glucose value)  
* Single continuous line for 24-hour period

Week view:

* Group measurements by day of week (Sun-Sat)  
* Normalize timestamps to time-of-day (0-24 hours) for X-axis overlay  
* Render 7 separate lines with different colors  
* Each line shows one day's pattern overlaid on same 24-hour timeline  
* Include legend to show day labels

### Empty State

Update empty state handling in both landing card and details view to ensure that when measurements are empty, we show the "No data for \[BGM/CGM\]" message. The toggle should remain visible to allow switching between device types

### Summary Statistics

Update summary statistics calculations in DetailsView.tsx to pass deviceType to isGlucoseInRange() when computing in-range count. Ensure average and in-range calculations use device type specific reference ranges.

# Testing Strategy

Unit tests should be written for each code change per best practice. This section focuses on expected updates to integration testing:

## Healthdata Backend

* Verify that we can create FHIR Observation with CGM LOINC code (97507-8)  
* Verify ListObservations can return CGM observations  
* Set up test patient with both BGM and CGM data

## Patient BFF

Ensure that:

* Mock healthdata responses can have mixed observations  
* ListGlucoseMeasurements with device\_type=BGM only returns BGM observations  
* ListGlucoseMeasurements with device\_type=CGM only returns CGM observations  
* ListGlucoseMeasurements without device\_type returns both BGM and CGM  
* Same for ListAggregatedGlucoseMeasurements

## Patient MFE

Add tests that:

* Toggle between BGM/CGM views on landing card  
* Toggle between BGM/CGM views on details view  
* Verify correct chart type renders for each view (scatter vs line)  
* Verify stats update correctly per device type (reference ranges, in-range counts)  
* Verify month view is disabled when CGM is selected  
* Verify auto-switch from month to week when toggling to CGM  
* Verify toggle visibility with various data scenarios (BGM only, CGM only, both, neither)
